item.birch_n_bees.wildflower_twine.descr: §7A bundle of twine crafted from stems of §2wildflowers. §7It's rather crude, but still strong enough for tying stuff together.
item.birch_n_bees.stripped_wildflower_tw:
§7All irregular lumps and blooms of the twine has been carefully cut away. It didn't do very much to improve the product...
However, too much material was removed to be useful anymore.
item.birch_n_bees.wildthread.description: §7The sugar crystallized into a flexible and oddly tough coating, resulting in a rather durable thread that should be much more versatile than the unrefined twine it was made of
item.birch_n_bees.birch_bark.description: §7Papery bark peeled off of a birch tree. Probably has a few uses, but §6it's too dry right now...
item.birch_n_bees.soggy_birch_bark.descr: §7The papery bark is now §9sopping WET. §7It's more useful now.
item.birch_n_bees.splinters.description: §7Sharp splinters from an axe's miserable attempts to destroy a wooden log.
item.birch_n_bees.bundle_of_splinters.de: §7Sharp splinters wrapped and held together by a piece of §9soggy bark. §7Seems nonsensical, but there's a method to this madness.
item.birch_n_bees.three_sugarcane.descri:
§7It's three sugarcane. 
In a row. 
What? did you really think it would be that easy to get paper? 

idiot.
item.birch_n_bees.raw_sugar.description: §7An unrefined, sticky mass of raw cane sugar. The drying process was uneven and poor; half of it by weight is residual water, not the actual sugar crystals...
item.birch_n_bees.clay_brick.description: §7Clay shaped into the form of a brick. It's too wet to be of use, §6maybe try tempering it by putting it near§c something hot...?
item.birch_n_bees.wildflower_dressing.de: §7A shoddy wound dressing crafted by mixing mashed wildflowers with honey. Could be used to §6disinfect §4wounds §7and §6cure §2poison.
item.birch_n_bees.wildflower_bandage.des: §7A bandage fashioned out of wildflower twine with a honey-based dressing smeared on it to act as an antipoison. It'll have to do.
item.birch_n_bees.wildflower_popper.desc: §7Made with sugar, spice, and §meverything nice§r§7 a little bit of gunpowder, this cute little firecracker can still cause a bit of damage despite its small size!
item.birch_n_bees.honey_axe.description: §7A crude axe constructed of §6honeycomb §7and twigs, tied together with §2wildflower twine. §7It'll probably fall apart soon but you might be able to get some use out of it...
item.birch_n_bees.honey_shovel.descripti: §7A questionable shovel manufactured out of §6honeycomb §7and twigs, strung tight with §2wildflower twine. §7Now you can dig up clay and dirt without having to use your hands.
item.birch_n_bees.crude_cobblestone_pick: §7A shoddy pickaxe cobbled together from pebbles, tip reinforced with a sharp flint rock to allow it to chip away at stone.
§7An axe, made of loose stone and poorly sharpened flint, haphazardly bound to a twig. Not your proudest handiwork... It's wayy too blunt to properly cut anything, but you could use it to give a concussion in a pinch.
item.birch_n_bees.crude_cobblestone_swor: §7...really puts the "improvised" in "improvised weaponry". I mean, if it's not broken, don't fix it...?
item.birch_n_bees.flint_knife.descriptio:
§7A flimsy knife not-so expertly crafted out of a chunk of flint.
It's just sharp enough §6to cut things...
item.birch_n_bees.birch_shell.descriptio: §7A large, tough chunk of birch bark §6rarely dropped by some select birch monsters. §7This tough pseudo-carapace is rather durable for being made of birch bark... maybe you could §6fashion it into a shield...?
item.birch_n_bees.birch_shield.descripti: §7A glorified piece of birch wood refashioned into something that could be mistaken as a shield
birch_n_bees.message.wood_too_hard: §6Your tainted hand is not worthy enough to break our precious trees.
birch_n_bees.message.stone_too_hard: §7This stone seems too hard to break... §6Maybe find a softer rock first?
birch_n_bees.message.empty_offhand: §7You need a tool head to build a tool!
birch_n_bees.message.no_offhand: §7Tool head in offhand, tool base in main hand
birch_n_bees.message.no_offhand_thread: §7Thread in offhand, spool in main hand
birch_n_bees.message.incorrect_tool_knif: §7You need a §6knife §7to harvest this! §6[Shift] §4to break §7if you are certain.
birch_n_bees.message.unlawful_animal_kil: §4SLAUGHTER OF ANIMALS BY AN IMPURE IS STRICTLY FORBIDDEN.
advancements.ballad.root.title: §6A Ballad of Birch and Bees!
advancements.ballad.root.description: Welcome to Hell. Enjoy Your Stay!
advancements.ballad.welcome.title: CHECK ACHIEVEMENT PAGE!!!!
advancements.ballad.welcome.description:
§7This achievement page will act as a form of guide. Try to follow this to the best of your abilities, else you might get stuck... 
Installing JEI is HEAVILY recommended

Pinky promise I won't try to get you killed ;)
advancements.ballad.go_wood.title: It's Minecraft. Go Punch a Tree!
advancements.ballad.go_wood.description: §7Attempt to mine some wood. Go on, it's perfectly fine and safe. You won't die or anything.
advancements.ballad.broken_promise.title: I lied.
advancements.ballad.broken_promise.descr:
§7First rule of the birch forest: If someone says they are going to help you, they are not going to help you.
Second rule: bees are not your friend.
advancements.ballad.axe_todo.title: How to (Actually) Progress
advancements.ballad.axe_todo.description: §7Ok, but do you actually want to progress? Fine, I'll tell you. Mining wood isn't that simple, you'll need a proper tool... I got an idea. Go gather a few items for me, will ya?
advancements.ballad.sweet_steps.title: The Great Honey Heist
advancements.ballad.sweet_steps.descript: §7You need some honeycomb before you can craft your first tool. Strange, I know. Go destroy one of the many beehives dotting the forest. You need 3 in total.
advancements.ballad.twine.title: Flower Crowns, Anyone?
advancements.ballad.twine.description:
§7We need something to "tie" (pun intended) all of this together... Go grab some of those wildflowers and craft some twine with it.
[4 in a 2x2 grid]
advancements.ballad.brown_and_sticky.des:
§7A stick.

Go break leaves until you get one. Saplings can be crafted into sticks if you get one of those first.
advancements.ballad.axe_done.title: New Tool?
advancements.ballad.axe_done.description:
§7...You actually did it? Without dying?
§8Damn, now I owe 20 bucks...
§7...Anyway, now you can craft §6Honey Tools§7...! Go do that.
[honeycomb + twine + stick]
advancements.ballad.tool_base_intro.titl: Using Tool Bases for Idiots Like YOU
advancements.ballad.tool_base_intro.desc:
§7Crafting tools will require a bit more work than that. You've crafted yourself a §6Honey Tool Base§7, but you wont get very far without a tool head... Craft a §6Honey Axe Head§7, put it in your offhand, and right-click with the tool base. 
[two comb placed vertically]
advancements.ballad.honey_axe_get.title: §6A "Sweet" New Axe!
advancements.ballad.honey_axe_get.descri: §7Craft a §6Honey Axe! §7Now you can probably go mine a tree without dying.
advancements.ballad.honey_shovel_get.des: §7It is possible to craft a few more tools using §6Honey Tool Bases§7... Most notably, you'll want to craft yourself a §6Honey Shovel§7 to gather some clay and flint.
advancements.ballad.splinters.descriptio:
§7Life isn't fair, huh? Logs just break into splinters when broken. They're probably still useful. Go grab a few more.
P.S. you can craft them into sticks if you don't want to punch more leaves.
advancements.ballad.stripping_bark.title: Don't Bark Up the Wrong Tree
advancements.ballad.stripping_bark.descr: §7Using an axe on a birch log strips it. There's a chance you can get some birch bark if you do that. You'll need a lot of this stuff...
advancements.ballad.sogging_bark.title: I LOVE My Bark §9Sopping W E T
advancements.ballad.sogging_bark.descrip:
§7Dunking your birch bark into water makes it all §9wet and SOGGY.§7 Use it to bundle splinters together 
[3x splinter, 1x bark] 

Oh. And you can eat it. If you want to do that, for some reason.
advancements.ballad.bundling_splinters.d:
§7Soggy birch bark and 3 splinters equals...! 
...A bundle of splinters. Held together by birch bark. Debatably useful... You're going to need at least 12 of these btw. So get to chopping!
advancements.ballad.carpenter.title: This Would Probably Feel Horrible to Walk On :(
advancements.ballad.carpenter.descriptio:
§7Some honey to act as glue allows you to stick 3 bundles of splinters together to get exactly one (1) sticky plank. Annoying to craft, huh? You need 3 more :-)
[craft with 4 in a 2x2 grid to progress]
advancements.ballad.birching_table.title: Tedium Ad Nauseam
advancements.ballad.birching_table.descr:
§7You could be excused if you assumed this was a §l§oCrafting Table.§r§7 
Well, I wont excuse you, but someone else could.

This, my friend, is a §l§oBIRCHING Table.§r§7
advancements.ballad.snap_crackle_pop.des:
§7To reach the stone age, you need to craft a cute little explosive known as a §6Wildflower Popper§7. It requires gunpowder and string, however...

I wouldn't recommend crafting a wooden pickaxe, by the way.
advancements.ballad.digging_earth.title: Using Your Shovel
advancements.ballad.digging_earth.descri:
§7You'll need to find some clay and gravel. Dig it up with your shovel to get some clay and flint.
§5Clay and flint will only drop if broken with a shovel.
§7They also both drop less and less frequently in order to annoy you.
advancements.ballad.sharpest_tool.title: Not the Sharpest Tool in the Shed...
advancements.ballad.sharpest_tool.descri: §7Craft yourself a Flint Knife! §7It's rather dull, but might be §6sharp enough to cut through reeds...
advancements.ballad.reeds_of_destony.des: §6Sugar Cane §7can only be gathered with §6knives or other sharp tools. §7You'll need to gather a few in order to progress.
advancements.ballad.sun_drying.title: Basics in Sugar Processing: Step 2!
advancements.ballad.sun_drying.descripti:
§7The next step in sugar cane processing is drying it out: §6hold right-click §7with your §6knife §7in one hand and the cane in the other to prepare it for drying--- then §6place it in the sun and wait...
§7You'll see little sugar crystals form on the cane once it's ready!
advancements.ballad.sweet_victory.title: Sweet, Sweet Progress!
advancements.ballad.sweet_victory.descri: §7Once your prepared sugar cane is done drying, you should have access to §6Raw Sugar!
advancements.ballad.better_flower_crowns: §7Those crudely made flower crowns of yours worked to tie your honey tools together, but it wont get you much farther than that. Luckily, you can refine §2Wildflower Twine §7further via a §6knife§7--- get cutting!
advancements.ballad.spools.title: This Wont Take Ages.
advancements.ballad.spools.description: §7After stripping down the twine to its bare essentials, you are ready to refine it properly. §6Craft it with a stick and some raw sugar §7(I hope you have plenty of it drying out!) and prepare for a long night of holding right click ahead of you...
advancements.ballad.finger_cramps.title: Awugh, My Finger's Cramping...
advancements.ballad.finger_cramps.descri: §7If your fingers feel up to more progression, you can finally use your newly wound spool of §2Wildthread §7to craft more tools! You'll need a §2Wildthread Tool Base §7to access the next tier of tools, including Crude Cobblestone Tools once you're brave enough to venture out at night and craft yourself a §6Wildflower Popper.
advancements.illegal.root.title: §4Illegal Actions
advancements.illegal.root.description: Some actions have been deemed ILLEGAL and against the LAW to commit! This page details any crimes you're guilty of.
advancements.illegal.wood.title: §4Attempt to Mine Wood
advancements.illegal.wood.description: §7Using your DISGUSTING, FILTHY hands to try to break our PRISTINE logs is §4NOT ALLOWED.
advancements.illegal.hunting.title: §4You Got a License for That?
advancements.illegal.hunting.description: §7It is §4ILLEGAL §7to hunt and kill animals without a hunting license.
advancements.illegal.contraband.title: §4§lCONTRABAND
advancements.illegal.contraband.descript: §7Some items are considered §4§lCONTRABAND§r§7 and will have you executed on the spot if you are found holding any.
advancements.dont_die.root.title: How to not Die (for Dummies Like You)
advancements.dont_die.root.description: §7Experts say dying isn't good for your health, apparently. Let's try to avoid doing that, shall we?
advancements.dont_die.antipoison_1.title: Not for Salads
advancements.dont_die.antipoison_1.descr: §7Honey is great, obviously. But did you know you can use it to cure poison? It's true. 1 honeycomb and 3 wildflowers gives you some §6Wildflower Dressing§7, which can be used as a quick and dirty antipoison!
advancements.dont_die.antipoison_2.title: For All Your Scrapes and Bruises
advancements.dont_die.antipoison_2.descr: §7Wouldn't it be nice if your §6Wildflower Dressing§7 could do more for ya? Well, if you have a Birching Table, you can surround 1 §6Wildflower Dressing§7 with 8 §2Wildflower Twine§7 to get a §6Wildflower Bandage!§7 Gives you a bit of regeneration to go with the antipoison.
advancements.dont_die.blocking_with_bark: Second Only to Cardboard in Terms of Protection
§7Night time is scary. But, necessary for progression, unfortunately. You can craft yourself a shoddy shield made of birch bark if you get lucky when killing a §6Splintering. §7That could help.
advancements.hostile_world.root.title: The World isn't so Safe
advancements.hostile_world.root.descript: §7The world is full of a bunch of hostile creatures! This is a cohesive list of all new or important entities.
advancements.hostile_world.bee.title: Float Like a Butterfly and Sting Like a...
advancements.hostile_world.bee.descripti: §7...Bee! The titular bee is now a very dangerous hazard. Bees are now always aggressive towards the player, and are frequently used to punished rule-breakers. Beware Killer Bees.
advancements.hostile_world.salmon.title: Fishy Business
advancements.hostile_world.salmon.descri:
§7Beware the frightful Salmranha! These half-salmon, half piranha flesh-eating monsters infest most rivers. They will aggressively attack anyone who dares enter their rivers.

Be VERY CAREFUL if it starts raining.
advancements.hostile_world.night.title: Fear of the dark
advancements.hostile_world.night.descrip: §7Nighttime is when most dangerous creatures emerge. Stay safe!
advancements.hostile_world.splintering.d:
§7The Splintering's explosion is much less impressive than its cousin, the creeper; however, beware: it fires out a spread of i-frame ignoring splinters upon detonation!

§6Splinterings are incapable of stopping their fuse once they get started... Maybe you can use that to your advantage?
advancements.hostile_world.nesthead.desc:
§7The Nesthead is a zombie with a bee nest stuck to their head... Makes you wonder how that happened. Nestheads will spawn aggressive bees during their combat encounter, releasing swarms when hurt. The sticky honey glued to their bodies will slow opponents they touch.

§6Nestheads don't have an infinite amount of bees and will eventually run out.
